message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>BACKGOUND</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The spread of Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) had been controlled in China. The seroprevalence of COVID-19 in Beijing has not been evaluated.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>METHODS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>In April, residents in Beijing were randomly enrolled. Blood samples were collected and antibodies to SARS- CoV-2 were tested by two colloidal gold kits. All colloidal gold positive serums were then tested by Micro- neutralization assay.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>RESULTS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>None of 2,184 residents participated was tested positive by micro-neutralization assay. The seroprevalence of COVID-19 in Beijing was estimated < 0.17%.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>CONCLUSIONS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The seroprevalence of COVID-19 was low in April suggesting that community-wide spread was prevented in Beijing.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: A population-based seroprevalence survey of severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 infection in Beijing, China

reference.article-title: Seroprevalence of SARS-CoV-2-Specific Antibodies Among Adults in Los Angeles County
